rationale: The citing paper uses Rao and Wang (2017) as part of a specific methodological argument: prior causal evidence on false claims often comes from policy changes that remove misinformation. That point is used to motivate the citing paper's main contribution, namely direct experimental exposure to misinformation and debunking with incentive-compatible purchase measures. So the citation helps frame the research gap and contribution, not just broad topical background. Even though it appears in grouped citations and without direct method/data reuse, its role is still meaningfully explanatory.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Rao and Wang (2017) as prior false-claims evidence based on policy-driven misinformation removal, then builds beyond it by testing direct exposure to misinformation and corrective debunking messages and their effects on WTP and beliefs.
